producer: QE 7.5 (conda) pw.x+ph.x electron_phonon='simple' @ vast.ai GPU pod + star-weighted BZ-sum reconstruction (lambda_BZ + lambda-weighted log-avg omega_log) + hexa verify allen_dynes_tc
novelty: H3X family pnictide (Group-15) heavy-As substitution — 5th in H3X sweep alongside h3o/h3cl/h3f/h3si/h3p; tests V1 §B8 mass-trend Tc ∝ 1/sqrt(m_X) for heavy pnictide
purpose: Parse the harvested H3As 6x6x6-q electron-phonon DFT run; persist BZ-summed lambda, omega_log, Allen-Dynes Tc (mu*=0.10 and 0.13), dynamical stability, and the M8 funnel evaluation. Tests heavy-pnictide H3X variant per RTSC H3X mass-trend prediction (V1 §B8).
system: H3As cubic Im-3m BCC primitive (1 As + 3 H = 4 atoms), ibrav=3, celldm(1)=6.700 bohr (a~3.545 Angstrom), unit-cell volume 150.38 (a.u.)^3, 8 valence electrons, Fermi energy 9.5376 eV

method.pseudopotentials: PBE ultrasoft pslibrary (As.pbe-rrkjus_psl.1.0.0, H.pbe-rrkjus_psl.1.0.0) — atomic-code v6.3
method.scf: ecutwfc/ecutrho per pslibrary RRKJUS recommendations, 413 k-points, Methfessel-Paxton smearing degauss=0.02 Ry; Fermi energy 9.5376 eV
method.phonon: ph.x DFPT, 6x6x6 q-grid (16 irreducible q-points), star multiplicities [4,12,12,12,12,12,8,8,12,12,12,8,12,8,12,4] sum=160 (same Im-3m convention as h3cl/h3f/h3si)
method.elph: electron_phonon='simple', el_ph_sigma=0.005, el_ph_nsigma=10 (10 Gaussian broadenings 0.005-0.050 Ry)
method.moments: BZ-weighted lambda_BZ = sum_q w_q sum_nu lambda(q,nu) / sum_q w_q (negative QE-printed lambda from imaginary modes RETAINED in numerator for honest BZ sum); omega_log via lambda-weighted log-average over positive-omega modes only (imaginary modes excluded from log-average, ln(neg) undefined)
method.job_status: ph.out contains 'JOB DONE.' (1 match) — run completed; PHONON wall 3h35m on vast GPU pod

phonon_frequencies_cm-1.note: 192 mode entries across 16 q-points (12 modes each = 4 atoms x 3). Imaginary modes spread across 12 of 16 q-points (only q=7, q=12, q=13 are fully real). Largest imaginary at q=16 (-1267 cm-1) — soft H-derived mode signaling a structural instability of the Im-3m H3As phase at this geometry.

stability.note: Severe dynamical instability — Im-3m H3As is NOT a local minimum at the parsed geometry. 25/192 modes imaginary including the entire Gamma triplet (-1244 cm-1) and most zone-boundary modes (-1267 cm-1 at q=16). Phase relaxation / lower-symmetry distortion (e.g. Pnma, R-3m, or Im-3) is needed before any Tc claim is meaningful.

results_by_broadening.note: small-broadening artifact (double-delta unconverged), NOT the converged value
headline broadening per SrAuH3/h3X convention; lambda monotone-increasing across the full 0.010-0.050 sweep — UNDER-CONVERGED (no true plateau)

result.convergence_note: lambda is monotone-increasing across the entire broadening sweep (0.010-0.050 Ry: 0.54 -> 1.22) — no true plateau, UNDER-CONVERGED. Headline 0.030 Ry chosen to mirror SrAuH3/h3o convention. True converged lambda likely lies between 0.6-1.2 with the structure-instability caveat dominating. STABILITY is the gating axis here — Tc number is moot until the phase relaxes to a dynamically-stable variant.

hexa_verify.verdict_mu010:
verify --expr allen_dynes_tc(0.9706,444.06,0.1)=29.5122
  calc   = 29.5122  ≈ expected 29.5122  (|Δ|=0.0 ≤ ε=1e-9)
  tier   = 🟢 SUPPORTED-NUMERICAL  (hexa-native libm-class recompute, TECS-L n6-rep Tier2)
hexa_verify.verdict_mu013:
verify --expr allen_dynes_tc(0.9706,444.06,0.13)=25.1639
  calc   = 25.1639  ≈ expected 25.1639  (|Δ|=0.0 ≤ ε=1e-9)
  tier   = 🟢 SUPPORTED-NUMERICAL  (hexa-native libm-class recompute, TECS-L n6-rep Tier2)

m8_evaluation.criterion: pressure < 50 GPa AND stable AND Tc > 200K (RTSC.md M8 / N5 ambient-stable funnel)
m8_evaluation.axis_pressure_lt_50GPa: N/A — this run carries no EOS/pressure record (fixed-cell elph only at celldm(1)=6.7 bohr). Pressure axis CANNOT be evaluated from this run; flagged for a separate EOS run if the phase is ever stabilized.
m8_evaluation.axis_stable: FAIL — 25 of 192 modes imaginary (min freq -1266.84 cm-1 at q=16; entire Gamma triplet imaginary at -1244 cm-1); 12 of 16 q-points carry at least one imaginary mode. Im-3m H3As is NOT dynamically stable at this geometry.
m8_evaluation.axis_Tc_gt_200K: FAIL — headline Tc(mu0.10)=29.51 K, Tc(mu0.13)=25.16 K; even the broadest 0.050 Ry value (Tc~39.5 K) is far below the 200K threshold. Tc number is moot anyway given the instability.
m8_evaluation.verdict: M8 NOT MET. 0 of 3 axes PASS (stability FAIL hard, Tc FAIL hard, pressure N/A). H3As Im-3m is a dynamically-unstable phase with weak-coupling Tc; does NOT advance the ambient-stable RTSC funnel. Heavy-pnictide V1 §B8 mass-trend prediction is irrelevant for the unstable phase — the dominant signal is structural, not electronic.

h3x_family_context.predicted_band_per_V1_B8: Tc ~ 1/sqrt(m_X) heavy-X trend would place H3As Tc between H3P (lighter) and H3Sb (heavier)
h3x_family_context.DFT_result_K: ~25-30K headline at 0.030 Ry broadening, but the phase is dynamically unstable so the number is moot
h3x_family_context.verdict_vs_prediction: INSTABILITY DOMINATES. The mass-trend prediction implicitly assumes a stable Im-3m phase; H3As at this geometry is not stable. A relaxed lower-symmetry phase (e.g. Pnma, R-3m) would be needed to test the trend honestly. Honest finding: heavy-pnictide H3X stoichiometry is not phase-stable in Im-3m at ambient — consistent with the chemical intuition that As is too large for the 4-atom Im-3m cell at this lattice parameter.
h3x_family_context.next_step: (a) EOS scan vs pressure to test pressure-stabilization, or (b) variable-cell relaxation to find a lower-symmetry ground state, before any new elph run is warranted.

honest_caveats: DOMINANT: dynamical instability — 25/192 imaginary modes including Gamma. Tc value is reported for completeness but is physically meaningless until the phase is stabilized.

lambda_BZ and omega_log were RECONSTRUCTED from per-mode lambda(nu)/gamma in ph.out + dyn-file frequencies via star-weighted BZ sum (lambda.x output absent from tgz; QE electron_phonon='simple' prints only per-q per-mode lambda). Method matches the SrAuH3 / h3X convention.
lambda is monotone-increasing across the full broadening sweep — strictly UNDER-CONVERGED. There is no plateau in 0.010-0.050 Ry; the 0.030 Ry headline is a convention pick, not a converged value.
DFT is Tier-1 PREDICTION, not Tier-3 measurement: gate_type=simulation-only-prediction, absorbed=false (R4 invariant).

Anharmonicity (often crucial for H-bearing systems with soft modes) was not included. SSCHA-level treatment could stabilize some imaginary modes via quantum-anharmonic corrections — common pattern for hydride high-Tc phases.
Coarse 6x6x6 q-grid; denser k/q would shift lambda and could partly soften/harden the imaginary modes, but it cannot rescue the Gamma instability (already on the grid).

provenance.dft_run_host: vast.ai GPU pod (provisioned via hexa cloud)
provenance.parser: /tmp/h3as/parse.py (linear ph.out scan + dyn freq extraction + star-weighted BZ sum)

r4_invariant: absorbed=false · gate_type=simulation-only-prediction · domain=material. DFT prediction record; H3As Im-3m is dynamically unstable + weak-coupling Tc → does NOT claim RTSC absorbed=true (M8 not met — stability FAIL, Tc FAIL, pressure N/A). No impossibility framing: the stable phase (lower-symmetry distortion or pressure-stabilized variant) remains an open breakthrough path for the heavy-pnictide H3X branch.
